author: Mark Frost
text: 2026-07-17 code implemented + validated (soak-safe, hardware enablement deferred). New module lichen/subsys/lichen/link/epoch_persist.c: lichen_link_epoch_advance_for_boot() loads the previous boot's epoch from Zephyr settings (NVS), advances by one with 8-bit wrap, persists it, and returns it — idempotent within a boot. Wired into both lichen_link_init() call sites in lichen_l2.c via lichen_link_set_epoch(), gated CONFIG_LICHEN_LINK_EPOCH_PERSIST (depends on SETTINGS; default off). The +1 advance keeps the node's (epoch<<16|seq) counter one epoch ahead of the peer's remembered replay counter; the window's half-space RFC1982 arithmetic treats +1 as 'ahead' even across the 255->0 wrap, so no receiver change is needed and the reboot coin-flip is eliminated. Validated: new lichen/tests/link_epoch (native_sim, 4/4) covers increment, 255->0 wrap, within-boot idempotency, and monotonic-across-3-simulated-reboots (the key persist+reload case); link_crypto + ping_l2 pass unchanged; t_echo/nrf52840 puck builds+links with the config forced on (board has the flash/settings partition). PENDING: enable CONFIG_LICHEN_LINK_EPOCH_PERSIST (+SETTINGS/NVS/FLASH) in the bench board confs (t_echo, t1000_e, heltec gateway — need to confirm each has a settings partition) and hardware-validate that a single-node reboot no longer causes one-way replay deafness. Deferred until the 12h soak frees the bench.
